prediction: The regular City Council meeting is scheduled for Tuesday, March 17, 2026, as stated in Item 1 of the agenda.
The applicant on the conditional use permit in CUP 24-031 is Coastal Surf School, LLC. This information is found in the "Applicant" section of the Planning Commission file.
The maximum building height permitted in the RM-2 zone, according to the zoning code excerpt, is 30 feet. This can be found in section § 17.32.040 A.

The total appropriation requested in the FY 2026-27 mid-year budget adjustment, per Item 9, is $2,450,000. This includes $1,800,000 for emergency storm drain repairs and $650,000 for Public Works overtime.
According to the Public Comment Summary for the Short-Term Rental Ordinance (Item 12), 113 letters were received in opposition.

Per General Plan Goal LU-3, the city's policy on mixed-use development along El Camino Real includes the following, as specified in Policies LU-3.1 and LU-3.2:

- Policy LU-3.1: Allow ground-floor commercial with residential above, up to 3 stories.
- Policy LU-3.2: Require pedestrian-oriented design and street-facing entries.

The vote tally on the motion to approve the consent calendar at the February 18 meeting was 5-0. All councilmembers who voted (Ramirez, Chen, Patel, Goldstein, and Mayor Whitfield) approved the motion.

The staff report recommends that the project under GPA 25-002 is exempt from CEQA under State CEQA Guidelines § 15061(b)(3) (common sense exemption). This is based on the determination that there is no possibility the activity may have a significant effect on the environment.
The deadline for filing a written appeal of the Planning Commission's decision is within 10 calendar days of the date of decision. According to Item 7, this specific date is March 5, 2026.

The contract amount recommended in the staff report for the street sweeping services agreement is not to exceed $1,275,000 over the term. The term recommended is a 3-year agreement with two optional one-year renewals at the City Manager's discretion. This information is found in the "Recommendation" section of the Street Sweeping Services Agreement.
The maximum building height permitted in the SF-3 zoning district is 35 feet, as stated in § 25-2-492(A)(2) of the Austin Land Development Code.
Council Member Velásquez (District 3) sponsored Resolution 20260319-042. You can find this information in the "Sponsor" section of the provided context.

The application fee for a Specific Use Permit (SUP) in the Houston staff report excerpt is $1,250. This can be found in the "Application Fee" section, which states: "Application Fee: $1,250 (paid March 4, 2026, Receipt #HOU-2026-2210)".

Resolution R-2026-058 from the Cuyahoga County, Ohio agenda authorizes the County Executive to enter into a contract with North Coast Environmental LLC for remediation services at the former metals processing site located at 1820 Industrial PKWY. The contract amount is not to exceed $4,200,000. This information can be found in the "A RESOLUTION AUTHORIZING THE COUNTY EXECUTIVE TO ENTER INTO A CONTRACT WITH NORTHCOAST ENVIRONMENTAL LLC IN AN AMOUNT NOT TO EXCEED $4,200,000 FOR REMEDIATION SERVICES AT THE FORMER METALS PROCESSING SITE LOCATED AT 1820 INDUSTRIAL PKWY" section.

The vote tally on the New York City Council Intro 0412-2026 was 41 in favor, 7 opposed, with 3 absent. This information can be found in the "Stated Meeting Minutes" section of the March 12, 2026 meeting.

The term length and renewal structure for the Austin city manager contract, as per ITEM 4 — Employment Agreement, is an initial 4-year term, commencing May 1, 2026, with two optional 2-year renewals at the discretion of the Council.

The environmental review process that applies to the Brooklyn rezoning per the staff report is the City Environmental Quality Review (CEQR) procedure. This is explicitly stated in the section titled "Environmental Review," which mentions that a Final Environmental Impact Statement (FEIS) was issued under the CEQR procedure on February 12, 2026.

The recommended fund source for the Cook County, Illinois Board's emergency snow-removal supplemental appropriation is the General Fund unrestricted reserve (Fund 1100), as per the Comptroller's memo dated February 28, 2026. This can be found in Item 26-0214.

According to the Phoenix General Plan Amendment GPA 26-014, the new maximum residential density is 25 dwelling units per acre (du/ac). This is reflected in the proposed designation of High Density Residential.

Seattle Council Bill 121-2026 relates to enforcing the minimum wage and minimum compensation provisions of Title 14 SMC. Specifically, it expands the Office of Labor Standards' authority to issue civil penalties up to $20,000 per violation.

The assessed valuation cap proposed in the Travis County school-district item is not explicitly stated in the given context. The context mentions a proposed increase in the homestead exemption from $40,000 to $48,000, but it does not specify an assessed valuation cap. Therefore, I don't know the exact assessed valuation cap from the provided information.

The deadline for written objections is no later than 5:00 PM on April 7, 2026, which is 48 hours before the hearing. This information can be found in the section mentioning "Written objections must be submitted to the Office of Zoning and Development no later than 5:00 PM on April 7, 2026 (48 hours before the hearing)."
The maximum lot coverage for the Boston B-2 zoning district is 70%, as stated in section (b) of Article 53, § 53-13 of the BOSTON ZONING CODE.

The Denver City Council ordinance authorizes a five-year contract with Axon Enterprise, Inc. for the replacement and ongoing support of police body-worn cameras. The amount authorized does not exceed $9,800,000 over the term of the contract. This is specified in the "AN ORDINANCE" section of Bill 26-0188.

The public-comment period cited in the Portland staff report for the Comprehensive Plan amendment CP-26-007 is from January 15, 2026, to April 30, 2026, at 5:00 PM. This information is found under the "Public-comment period" section of the provided context.
